spec_match.rationale: Double-counts entries on supported browsers because it unconditionally calls the recordEntry simulation inline and also listens for real events via the PerformanceObserver, leading to corrupted visits and averages metrics.

interactive.rationale: Engaging dashboards displaying visits, avg/last durations with responsive visual latency bars.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned with the RUM Dashboard description.

edge_cases.rationale: Performs feature detection, but fails to isolate the simulated path from the real observer pipeline.

references.rationale: Violates repository hard rule: does not contain a link to chromestatus.com/feature/5144837209194496.

design_system.rationale: Fits beautifully with custom micro-charts conforming to showcase guidelines.

openQuestions.title: Isolate the simulation pathway and fix the double-recording bug
openQuestions.detail: On browsers supporting soft-navigation, the page double-counts every click by recording both the simulated payload inline and the real performance entry afterward. Refactor navigate() to only call recordEntry(...) with mock details when supported is false.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus link (hard rule violation)
openQuestions.detail: The page does not contain a link to chromestatus.com/feature/5144837209194496, which violates the repository hard rule that every concept page must include a ChromeStatus link.
